title: The #1 Role Your Content Team Needs in 2026 Is a Managing Editor
tldr: As AI makes content cheap to produce, editorial judgment becomes the differentiator — and a managing editor is the role to make that judgment scale. A clear argument for where human value lives in AI-era content ops.
why_fresh: reframes team structure around AI's impact
angle: Why editorial judgment is the scarce skill when content production is commoditized.

title: Your Best-Ranked Page Might Be Invisible to Google's AI
tldr: Ranking in Google's top 10 no longer guarantees visibility in AI Overviews, which cite differently than organic results. A wake-up call for how content discovery is shifting.
why_fresh: new visibility paradigm for AI search
angle: How to audit content for AI citation, not just SEO rank.

title: The Rundown: AI clones split the creator economy
tldr: Unauthorized AI voice clones and authorized digital twins are dividing creators, brands and lawyers over consent and control. A defining tension in synthetic media and talent rights.
angle: The consent economy: who owns a creator's synthetic likeness.

title: The Future of Content Belongs to the Tastemakers
tldr: With AI making polished copy trivial, taste and curation become the real differentiators for memorable content. Argues authenticity and judgment outlast scale.
angle: Why taste is the new competitive advantage in an AI-saturated feed.

title: Why 'AI Productivity Gains' Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Pitching AI as productivity gains won't move CMOs, CFOs or legal — reframe the metric per audience to unlock budget. Practical internal-comms framing for AI investment.
angle: How to translate AI value into each C-suite stakeholder's language.

title: What kind of stories turn local news readers into subscribers? Hard news, not soft
tldr: New data shows hard news — government, public health, politics — converts subscribers far better than soft lifestyle content. Counterintuitive evidence for editorial strategy.
angle: What media subscription data teaches brands about which content actually earns loyalty.

title: LinkedIn's Algorithm Killed Company Pages. Employees Are The Distribution Now.
tldr: LinkedIn's algorithm has gutted company-page reach, pushing brands toward employee-generated distribution. A direct hit to owned-channel and EGC strategy.
angle: Building an employee-as-distribution model now that company pages are dead.

title: The Anti-AI Brand Is Becoming a Real Market Position
tldr: As AI content floods every channel, 'human-made' is emerging as a deliberate brand differentiator. A new positioning lever worth watching.
angle: Is 'human-made' the next premium brand signal?

title: Why communicators are trading employee engagement for employee experience
tldr: Internal comms is moving from vanity engagement metrics to measuring real business outcomes, especially for frontline workers. A meaningful shift in how internal comms proves value.
angle: From clicks to outcomes: rebuilding internal comms measurement.

title: A 6-step framework for boosting social GEO
tldr: Practical framework for creating social content that AI platforms want to cite, as consumers shift discovery to AI tools. Actionable for the generative-search era.
angle: How to make brand social content citable by AI engines.

title: How to Write Content That Lands With Decision Makers
tldr: Output is up but senior buyers are less impressed — a practical guide for B2B thought leadership that actually moves contract-signers. Connects content craft to commercial impact.
why_fresh: solid but familiar B2B craft advice
angle: Why more content lowers your credibility with executives.

title: Your global strategy is broken if it starts with English
tldr: Defaulting to English-first product, campaigns and expansion quietly constrains global growth. A strong argument for transcreation and local-first content.
angle: Why English-first content architecture caps your global reach.

title: Hooked on the Scroll: How Vertical Dramas are Changing Brand Storytelling
tldr: Short vertical drama series are emerging as a new branded-content format, reshaping how brands tell stories on mobile. A fresh format trend for short-form video.
angle: Should brands invest in vertical micro-dramas as a storytelling format?

title: Podcasts and influencers least culturally relevant media
tldr: Thinkbox study finds TV drives the largest share of cultural influence while podcasts and creators rank lowest — a provocative counter to creator-economy hype.
angle: Are we overrating creators' actual cultural impact?

title: AI disclosure labels don't hurt ad performance: Here's what the numbers say
tldr: As AI-disclosure laws spread, new data shows labeling AI use in ads doesn't reduce performance. Useful evidence for brands navigating transparency.
angle: Why AI disclosure may build trust without costing performance.
